title: X-45 sniper rifle
content:
{{Top|ga|canon=X-45}} {{Weapon |image=[[File:X-45_Sniper_Rifle_SWJ3.png]] |name=X-45 sniper rifle
"Riflette" |manufacturer=[[SoroSuub Corporation/Legends|SoroSuub Corporation]]{{CSWECite|III|339|X-45}} |model=X-45 |type=*[[Sporting blaster rifle]]''[[Arms & Equipment Guide]]'' *[[Sniper rifle/Legends|Sniper rifle]]''[[Gundark's Fantastic Technology: Personal Gear]]'' |culture= |creators= |created= |destroyed= |discovered= |owners= |locations= |cost=750 to 1,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] |value= |shape= |size=Medium |material= |length= |width= |diam= |height= |weight=4 [[Kilogram/Legends|kilograms]] |protection= |crew= |capacity=25 shots |range=500 [[Meter/Legends|meters]]According to ''[[Arms & Equipment Guide]]'', the X-45 has a range increment of 50 meters. ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game]]'' uses the [[Wikipedia:d20 System|d20 roleplaying system]], in which projectile weapons normally have a maximum range of ten range increments. |inscription= |markings= |purpose= |heritage= |affiliation= }} {{Quote|Back when I was for hire as 'freelance troubleshooter' I used this sniper rifle a great deal. I discovered that if you take the targeting package from a security droid—or other fourth degree unit—and patch it into the computerized scope, it improves accuracy (which is already considerable) by as much as 30 percent.|A comment left by Aiden in ''Gundark's Gear Datalog''|}} The '''X-45 sniper rifle''' was a highly accurate [[Sporting blaster/Legends|sporting]] [[Sniper rifle/Legends|sniper rifle]] produced by the [[SoroSuub Corporation/Legends|SoroSuub Corporation]]. The X-45 was available from as early as the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], throughout the rule of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], and into the administration of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. One of the few downsides of the weapon was its fragility, as it had to be carried in a special case and only extracted when needed. The bolt it fired was thinner than a normal [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] bolt but was much more [[Death/Legends|lethal]], a result of its being overfocused in the [[Barrel/Legends|barrel]]. The weapon was initially marketed as a civilian weapon called the '''Riflette''', but the X-45 quickly became popular with [[Assassin/Legends|assassins]] and was re-categorized for military use. ==Description== [[File:Soro-Suub.svg|thumb|left|250px|The X-45 was manufactured by SoroSuub.]] The X-45 [[Sniper rifle/Legends|sniper rifle]], manufactured by the [[SoroSuub Corporation/Legends|SoroSuub Corporation]], was a medium-sized [[sporting blaster rifle]] noted for its high accuracy, especially if the target was within a fifty-[[Meter/Legends|meter]] range and the shooter fired in controlled single shots. The energy beam it fired was overfocused in the barrel, which resulted in a thinner but much more [[Death/Legends|lethal]] [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] bolt. Using the X-45 allowed the shooter to pick off his target without drawing too much attention.''[[Yoda: Dark Rendezvous]]'' The rifle had a long{{JournalCite|3|Blasters for Hire}} [[Computer/Legends|computerized]] [[targeting scope]] that was nearly the same length as the X-45's body, a stock, and a clip capacity of twenty-five shots. The X-45 weighed four [[Kilogram/Legends|kilograms]] and had a maximum range of 500 meters. The rifle could be disassembled and put into a specially made case. The X-45's major drawback was its extreme fragility, so the rifle was normally carried in the case until it was needed. The entire process of unpackaging and assembling the rifle took some [[Time/Legends|time]].''[[Ultimate Adversaries]]'' The X-45 cost between 750 and 1,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]]. ==History== The X-45 sniper rifle was SoroSuub's attempt to carve out its share of the sporting blaster market. The sniper rifle was developed at some point before the [[21 BBY/Legends|second year]] of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. During that time, a group of adventurers known as the [[Heroes of Cularin]] were investigating a murder that had occurred on the resort [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of [[Dorumaa]] in the [[Cularin system/Legends|Cularin system]]. [[Echari Di'San]], the [[President/Legends|President]] of the [[Cularin Trade Alliance]], was accused of killing a man named [[Rysko Barnt]] in the local [[Cantina Oceana]]. Determined to help Di'San prove his innocence, the Heroes of [[Cularin/Legends|Cularin]] tried to meet a [[Waiter/Legends|waiter]] called [[Hukno Rit]], who could testify on Di'San's behalf. However, when they arrived to pick Rit up, they found him dead and were attacked by a well-trained strike team armed with X-45 sniper rifles. The strike team had been dispatched by [[Thon Larimhor]], the man who had set Di'San up. The Heroes of Cularin survived the encounter and eventually managed to expose Larimhor. [[File:GundarksGearDatalog-GundarksFantasticTechnology.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The logo of the arms dealer Gundark, who sold the X-45]] Advertised as a sporting blaster rifle called the "Riflette," the X-45 was released to civilian markets during the regime of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. The accurate and commonly available rifle quickly became popular with [[Assassin/Legends|assassins]], so it was eventually reclassified as a military weapon. During the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic's]] rule over [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], the X-45 sniper rifle was included in ''[[Gundark's Gear Datalog]]'', an illegal database run by the notorious [[Arms dealer/Legends|arms dealer]] [[Gundark (arms dealer)|Gundark]]. The database included annotations from [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]], thieves, [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]], and other outlaws on some of the items. A note filed under the X-45's entry, left by an individual with the screen name [[Aiden]] around [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]],Aiden's comment is followed by the [[Great ReSynchronization]] date of 45:8:5:95, which corresponds to [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]] on the [[Galactic Standard Calendar]]. stated that Aiden had often used the X-45 rifle while working as a "freelance troubleshooter." Aiden praised its accuracy, but also provided a note explaining how to increase it even more, up to thirty percent, by taking a targeting package from a [[Security droid/Legends|security droid]], or other similar fourth degree [[Droid/Legends|droid]] unit, and patching it into the X-45's computerized scope. ==Behind the scenes== The X-45 sniper rifle was first mentioned in "[[Blasters for Hire]]," an article written and illustrated by [[Tony Russo|Anthony P. Russo]] and published in [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 3|''Star Wars Adventure Journal'' 3]] on [[August 1]], [[1994]]. The rifle was subsequently mentioned in several other reference books, for both [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' and [[Wizards of the Coast]]'s ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game]]''. Its first mention in an [[in-universe]] work was in [[Sean Stewart]]'s [[2004]] novel ''[[Yoda: Dark Rendezvous]]''. The X-45 was subsequently featured in ''[[Plea Bargain]]'', a year five adventure in the [[Living Force (roleplaying campaign)|Living Force]] RPG campaign. In [[2008]], it received in an entry in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''. There are several discrepancies regarding the X-45 rifle. Although ''The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia'' states that the rifle was first produced during the reign of the Galactic Empire, ''Plea Bargain'' and ''Yoda: Dark Rendezvous'' see the rifle being used during the Clone Wars, years prior to the Empire's rule.{{LivingForce|Plea Bargain}} However, other sources only state that the rifle was released to civilian markets during the Empire's rule, not excluding the possibility of it being produced earlier. Additionally, "Blasters for Hire" and the ''[[Gundark's Fantastic Technology: Personal Gear]]'' reference book give the X-45's price as 750 credits. However, ''[[Arms & Equipment Guide]]'' and ''[[Ultimate Adversaries]]'' sourcebooks place the price at 1,000 credits. This article assumes that the rifle's price varied between these estimates. ==Appearances== *{{LivingForce|Plea Bargain}} {{1st}} *''[[Yoda: Dark Rendezvous]]'' {{Ab|Yoda: Dark Rendezvous (unabridged audiobook)}} {{Mo}} ==Sources== *{{JournalCite|3|Blasters for Hire}} {{1stm}} *''[[Gundark's Fantastic Technology: Personal Gear]]'' *{{GamerCite|9|Wraith Squadron: The Dirty Dozen of Star Wars|Wraith Squadron: The Dirty Dozen of ''Star Wars''}} *''[[Arms & Equipment Guide]]'' *''[[Ultimate Adversaries]]'' *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Blaster sniper rifles]] [[Category:SoroSuub Corporation products]] [[Category:Sporting blaster rifles]]
